messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following social media influencer marketing platform privacy policy for GDPR compliance:

"CreatorHub connects brands with influencers. We collect your name, social media handles, audience demographics, engagement rates, and bank details for payment. Data is used to match you with relevant brand campaigns. We share your profile with interested brands. Your data may be processed in the US by our parent company. You can close your account at any time."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [FAIL] No lawful basis stated for any processing activity.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Audience demographics may include inferred ethnic origin or religious beliefs depending on niche. This risk is not addressed.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Campaign matching is a clear and specific stated purpose.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] Bank details collection is justified by payment purposes, but audience demographics scope is not defined.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [FAIL] No retention period stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [FAIL] Only account closure (implied erasure) referenced. No other rights stated.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PARTIAL] Brands named as recipients but processor relationship not described.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [FAIL] US processing by parent company disclosed but no transfer safeguard mechanism stated.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (no lawful basis), Art. 5 (no retention period, demographics scope undefined), Art. 15-21 (rights absent), Art. 28 (brand disclosure relationship undefined), Art. 46 (US transfer safeguards absent), Art. 37 (DPO not addressed), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- State lawful basis for each processing activity
- Define exactly what audience demographic data is collected and justify necessity
- Add a retention period and post-account-closure erasure timeline
- Add full data subject rights section
- Clarify whether brands are controllers or processors and reference relevant agreements
- Reference US transfer safeguard mechanism (SCCs)
- Assess and appoint DPO if required
- Add breach notification commitment
